business.summary: An annual self-assessment of RMiT compliance provides the institution with a structured view of its compliance posture across all applicable policy requirements, enabling targeted remediation and demonstrating accountability to BNM.

requirements.requirement: Conduct a comprehensive annual self-assessment covering all applicable RMiT policy requirements, classifying each clause as fully compliant, partially compliant, or non-compliant with supporting evidence
requirements.rationale: RMiT mandates an annual self-assessment covering all applicable requirements; incomplete coverage leaves compliance gaps unidentified and creates regulatory exposure during BNM examinations

requirements.requirement: Ensure the self-assessment is conducted by personnel with sufficient knowledge of both RMiT requirements and the institution's technology operations to make accurate compliance determinations
requirements.rationale: Self-assessments conducted without adequate expertise produce unreliable compliance ratings that may either overstate readiness or fail to identify genuine compliance achievements

requirements.requirement: Present the completed self-assessment results to the board or senior management for review and sign-off, including a summary of compliance posture, key gaps, and recommended remediation priorities
requirements.rationale: Board review ensures senior leadership is aware of the institution's RMiT compliance posture and can allocate resources to address identified gaps

technology.summary: Technology teams must support the self-assessment process by providing accurate evidence of control implementation, configuration status, and operational metrics for each applicable RMiT clause.

requirements.requirement: Implement or utilise a GRC platform or compliance management tool to systematically track self-assessment progress, evidence collection, and compliance ratings across all RMiT clauses
requirements.rationale: A systematic tool-based approach ensures completeness of assessment, provides an audit trail, and enables efficient year-on-year comparison of compliance progress

requirements.requirement: Collect and organise technical evidence supporting compliance ratings for each assessed clause, including system configurations, control documentation, testing results, and operational metrics
requirements.rationale: BNM examiners expect supporting evidence for each compliance rating; assertions without evidence will be challenged and may be downgraded during examination

requirements.requirement: Generate comparative analytics showing compliance rating trends year-on-year across all RMiT domains to identify areas of improvement and areas of concern
requirements.rationale: Trend analysis demonstrates to BNM that the institution is actively improving its compliance posture rather than maintaining static compliance levels

governance.summary: Governance processes must define the self-assessment methodology, assign clear responsibilities for evidence collection and rating, and ensure results receive appropriate board-level review and action.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal RMiT Self-Assessment Methodology defining the assessment approach, compliance rating criteria (fully compliant, partially compliant, non-compliant), evidence requirements for each rating, and roles and responsibilities
requirements.rationale: A documented methodology ensures consistency and rigour in the assessment process and provides BNM examiners with confidence that the self-assessment was conducted systematically

requirements.requirement: Maintain a comprehensive evidence package for each self-assessment cycle, linking each compliance rating to specific supporting evidence that can be presented to BNM examiners on request
requirements.rationale: An evidence package transforms the self-assessment from a subjective exercise into a documented, defensible compliance record that withstands regulatory scrutiny

requirements.requirement: Document board or senior management review of the self-assessment results with formal sign-off, including any challenges raised and decisions on remediation priorities and resource allocation
requirements.rationale: Documented board review demonstrates governance oversight and creates accountability for addressing identified compliance gaps at the highest level of the institution

business.summary: Developing actionable remediation plans for all identified RMiT compliance gaps ensures that self-assessment findings translate into measurable improvements, with progress tracked and reported to the board at least quarterly.

requirements.requirement: Develop detailed remediation plans for every compliance gap identified in the annual RMiT self-assessment, specifying concrete actions, accountable owners, target completion dates, and required resources
requirements.rationale: RMiT mandates remediation plans for all identified gaps; plans without specific actions, owners, and dates are unenforceable and will not satisfy BNM examiners

requirements.requirement: Report remediation progress to the board at least quarterly, including status against target dates, newly completed remediations, overdue items, and any requests for additional resources or timeline extensions
requirements.rationale: Quarterly board reporting is an explicit RMiT requirement that ensures governance oversight of remediation progress and provides a forum for escalating blockers and resource constraints

requirements.requirement: Prioritise remediation actions based on risk severity, regulatory criticality, and potential impact on customers, ensuring critical gaps are addressed within six months and all gaps within the annual cycle
requirements.rationale: Risk-based prioritisation ensures limited resources are directed at the most significant compliance gaps first, reducing the institution's regulatory exposure as quickly as possible

technology.summary: Technology teams must execute their assigned remediation actions within agreed timelines, providing evidence of implementation and supporting verification testing to confirm gaps are genuinely closed.

requirements.requirement: Implement a remediation tracking system or utilise the GRC platform to manage all remediation actions with automated progress tracking, deadline alerts, and status reporting capabilities
requirements.rationale: Systematic tracking ensures no remediation action is lost or forgotten and enables accurate progress reporting to the board as required by RMiT

requirements.requirement: Provide technical implementation evidence for each completed remediation action, including configuration changes, test results, and before-and-after comparisons demonstrating the gap has been closed
requirements.rationale: Implementation evidence proves remediation is genuine and effective, not merely administrative closure; BNM examiners will request this evidence for completed actions

requirements.requirement: Conduct verification testing for completed technical remediations to confirm the control gap is genuinely closed and the implemented solution is functioning as intended in the production environment
requirements.rationale: Verification testing prevents premature closure of remediation items and ensures the fix actually resolves the compliance gap rather than merely addressing symptoms

governance.summary: Governance processes must ensure remediation plans are formally approved, progress is tracked against milestones, overdue items are escalated, and completion evidence is retained for regulatory examination.

requirements.requirement: Obtain formal board or senior management approval of the overall remediation plan including priorities, timelines, resource allocation, and acceptance of any residual risk for gaps with extended remediation timelines
requirements.rationale: Board approval creates accountability for remediation delivery and ensures resource commitments have governance backing

requirements.requirement: Escalate overdue remediation items to the board within 30 days of the target date breach, with a root cause analysis and revised timeline for completion
requirements.rationale: Timely escalation prevents remediation drift and ensures the board is aware of compliance gaps that are taking longer than planned to close

requirements.requirement: Maintain a complete remediation evidence package for each closed gap, linking the original self-assessment finding to the remediation plan, implementation evidence, and verification results
requirements.rationale: A complete evidence chain from finding to closure demonstrates the integrity of the remediation process and provides examination-ready documentation for BNM
